title: Group leader read/write BIFs
purpose: Every BEAM process has a group leader — a PID that receives I/O output. `io:format` sends messages to the group leader rather than writing to stdout directly. OTP's `init` and `application` modules read and set group leaders during startup. Without these BIFs, OTP code that manages I/O routing fails.
task: Add a group_leader field to Process and implement the `erlang:group_leader/0` and `erlang:group_leader/2` BIFs.

requirements.title: Group leader field on Process
requirements.spec: Add a `group_leader: Term` field to Process. The initial process (init) uses its own PID as group_leader. All spawned processes inherit their parent's group_leader.
acceptance: The initial process (init) has group_leader set to its own PID
Spawned processes inherit the parent's group_leader (NOT their own PID)
Test: spawn process, verify group_leader is parent's group_leader, not the child's own PID

requirements.title: group_leader/0 and group_leader/2 BIFs
requirements.spec: Register `erlang:group_leader/0` (returns current group leader) and `erlang:group_leader/2` (sets group leader for a process).
acceptance: `group_leader()` returns the calling process's group_leader PID
`group_leader(NewLeader, Pid)` sets the group leader for Pid, returns true
Setting group leader for a non-existent PID returns badarg
Test: get default group leader, set a new one, verify the change

create: crates/beamr/src/native/process_info_bifs.rs (add to existing if B-080 creates it, otherwise create)

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement full I/O protocol message routing — group_leader is stored but not used for I/O dispatch (beamr uses IoSink from B-039)
SHALL NOT implement group leader inheritance across spawn — spawned processes get the parent's group leader (simple copy)
SHALL use own PID as group_leader only for the initial (init) process — all spawned processes inherit from parent
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
